members.name: AddCustomMonitor
members.signature: public void AddCustomMonitor(StringName id, Callable callable, Array arguments, int type)
members.summary: Adds a custom monitor with the name [param id]. You can specify the category of the monitor using slash delimiters in [param id] (for example: [code]"Game/NumberOfNPCs"[/code]). If there is more than one slash delimiter, then the default category is used. The default category is [code]"Custom"[/code]. Prints an error if given [param id] is already present. [codeblocks] [gdscript] func _ready(): var monitor_value = Callable(self, "get_monitor_value") # Adds monitor with name "MyName" to category "MyCategory". Performance.add_custom_monitor("MyCategory/MyMonitor", monitor_value) # Adds monitor with name "MyName" to category "Custom". # Note: "MyCategory/MyMonitor" and "MyMonitor" have same name but different IDs, so the code is valid. Performance.add_custom_monitor("MyMonitor", monitor_value) # Adds monitor with name "MyName" to category "Custom". # Note: "MyMonitor" and "Custom/MyMonitor" have same name and same category but different IDs, so the code is valid. Performance.add_custom_monitor("Custom/MyMonitor", monitor_value) # Adds monitor with name "MyCategoryOne/MyCategoryTwo/MyMonitor" to category "Custom". Performance.add_custom_monitor("MyCategoryOne/MyCategoryTwo/MyMonitor", monitor_value) func get_monitor_value(): return randi() % 25 [/gdscript] [csharp] public override void _Ready() { var monitorValue = new Callable(this, MethodName.GetMonitorValue); // Adds monitor with name "MyName" to category "MyCategory". Performance.AddCustomMonitor("MyCategory/MyMonitor", monitorValue); // Adds monitor with name "MyName" to category "Custom". // Note: "MyCategory/MyMonitor" and "MyMonitor" have same name but different ids so the code is valid. Performance.AddCustomMonitor("MyMonitor", monitorValue); // Adds monitor with name "MyName" to category "Custom". // Note: "MyMonitor" and "Custom/MyMonitor" have same name and same category but different ids so the code is valid. Performance.AddCustomMonitor("Custom/MyMonitor", monitorValue); // Adds monitor with name "MyCategoryOne/MyCategoryTwo/MyMonitor" to category "Custom". Performance.AddCustomMonitor("MyCategoryOne/MyCategoryTwo/MyMonitor", monitorValue); } public int GetMonitorValue() { return GD.Randi() % 25; } [/csharp] [/codeblocks] The debugger calls the callable to get the value of custom monitor. The callable must return a zero or positive integer or floating-point number. Callables are called with arguments supplied in argument array.

members.name: GetCustomMonitor
members.summary: Returns the value of custom monitor with given [param id]. The callable is called to get the value of custom monitor. See also [method has_custom_monitor]. Prints an error if the given [param id] is absent.

members.name: GetCustomMonitorNames
members.summary: Returns the names of active custom monitors in an [Array].

members.name: GetCustomMonitorTypes
members.summary: Returns the [enum MonitorType] values of active custom monitors in an [Array].

members.name: GetMonitor
members.summary: Returns the value of one of the available built-in monitors. You should provide one of the [enum Monitor] constants as the argument, like this: [codeblocks] [gdscript] print(Performance.get_monitor(Performance.TIME_FPS)) # Prints the FPS to the console. [/gdscript] [csharp] GD.Print(Performance.GetMonitor(Performance.Monitor.TimeFps)); // Prints the FPS to the console. [/csharp] [/codeblocks] See [method get_custom_monitor] to query custom performance monitors' values.

members.name: GetMonitorModificationTime
members.summary: Returns the last tick in which custom monitor was added/removed (in microseconds since the engine started). This is set to [method Time.get_ticks_usec] when the monitor is updated.

members.name: HasCustomMonitor
members.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if custom monitor with the given [param id] is present, [code]false[/code] otherwise.

members.name: RemoveCustomMonitor
members.summary: Removes the custom monitor with given [param id]. Prints an error if the given [param id] is already absent.

values.name: AUDIO_OUTPUT_LATENCY
values.summary: Output latency of the [AudioServer]. Equivalent to calling [method AudioServer.get_output_latency], it is not recommended to call this every frame.

values.name: MEMORY_MESSAGE_BUFFER_MAX
values.summary: Largest amount of memory the message queue buffer has used, in bytes. The message queue is used for deferred functions calls and notifications. [i]Lower is better.[/i]

values.name: MEMORY_STATIC
values.summary: Static memory currently used, in bytes. Not available in release builds. [i]Lower is better.[/i]

values.name: MEMORY_STATIC_MAX
values.summary: Available static memory. Not available in release builds. [i]Lower is better.[/i]

values.name: MONITOR_MAX
values.summary: Represents the size of the [enum Monitor] enum.

values.name: NAVIGATION_2D_ACTIVE_MAPS
values.summary: Number of active navigation maps in the [NavigationServer2D]. This also includes the empty default navigation maps created by [World2D] instances.

values.name: NAVIGATION_2D_AGENT_COUNT
values.summary: Number of active navigation agents processing avoidance in the [NavigationServer2D].

values.name: NAVIGATION_2D_EDGE_CONNECTION_COUNT
values.summary: Number of polygon edges that are considered connected by edge proximity [NavigationServer2D].

values.name: NAVIGATION_2D_EDGE_COUNT
values.summary: Number of navigation mesh polygon edges in the [NavigationServer2D].

values.name: NAVIGATION_2D_EDGE_FREE_COUNT
values.summary: Number of navigation mesh polygon edges that could not be merged in the [NavigationServer2D]. The edges still may be connected by edge proximity or with links.

values.name: NAVIGATION_2D_EDGE_MERGE_COUNT
values.summary: Number of navigation mesh polygon edges that were merged due to edge key overlap in the [NavigationServer2D].

values.name: NAVIGATION_2D_LINK_COUNT
values.summary: Number of active navigation links in the [NavigationServer2D].

values.name: NAVIGATION_2D_OBSTACLE_COUNT
values.summary: Number of active navigation obstacles in the [NavigationServer2D].

values.name: NAVIGATION_2D_POLYGON_COUNT
values.summary: Number of navigation mesh polygons in the [NavigationServer2D].

values.name: NAVIGATION_2D_REGION_COUNT
values.summary: Number of active navigation regions in the [NavigationServer2D].

values.name: NAVIGATION_3D_ACTIVE_MAPS
values.summary: Number of active navigation maps in the [NavigationServer3D]. This also includes the empty default navigation maps created by [World3D] instances.

values.name: NAVIGATION_3D_AGENT_COUNT
values.summary: Number of active navigation agents processing avoidance in the [NavigationServer3D].

values.name: NAVIGATION_3D_EDGE_CONNECTION_COUNT
values.summary: Number of polygon edges that are considered connected by edge proximity [NavigationServer3D].

values.name: NAVIGATION_3D_EDGE_COUNT
values.summary: Number of navigation mesh polygon edges in the [NavigationServer3D].

values.name: NAVIGATION_3D_EDGE_FREE_COUNT
values.summary: Number of navigation mesh polygon edges that could not be merged in the [NavigationServer3D]. The edges still may be connected by edge proximity or with links.

values.name: NAVIGATION_3D_EDGE_MERGE_COUNT
values.summary: Number of navigation mesh polygon edges that were merged due to edge key overlap in the [NavigationServer3D].

values.name: NAVIGATION_3D_LINK_COUNT
values.summary: Number of active navigation links in the [NavigationServer3D].

values.name: NAVIGATION_3D_OBSTACLE_COUNT
values.summary: Number of active navigation obstacles in the [NavigationServer3D].

values.name: NAVIGATION_3D_POLYGON_COUNT
values.summary: Number of navigation mesh polygons in the [NavigationServer3D].

values.name: NAVIGATION_3D_REGION_COUNT
values.summary: Number of active navigation regions in the [NavigationServer3D].

values.name: NAVIGATION_ACTIVE_MAPS
values.summary: Number of active navigation maps in [NavigationServer2D] and [NavigationServer3D]. This also includes the empty default navigation maps created by [World2D] and [World3D] instances.

values.name: NAVIGATION_AGENT_COUNT
values.summary: Number of active navigation agents processing avoidance in [NavigationServer2D] and [NavigationServer3D].

values.name: NAVIGATION_EDGE_CONNECTION_COUNT
values.summary: Number of polygon edges that are considered connected by edge proximity [NavigationServer2D] and [NavigationServer3D].

values.name: NAVIGATION_EDGE_COUNT
values.summary: Number of navigation mesh polygon edges in [NavigationServer2D] and [NavigationServer3D].

values.name: NAVIGATION_EDGE_FREE_COUNT
values.summary: Number of navigation mesh polygon edges that could not be merged in [NavigationServer2D] and [NavigationServer3D]. The edges still may be connected by edge proximity or with links.

values.name: NAVIGATION_EDGE_MERGE_COUNT
values.summary: Number of navigation mesh polygon edges that were merged due to edge key overlap in [NavigationServer2D] and [NavigationServer3D].

values.name: NAVIGATION_LINK_COUNT
values.summary: Number of active navigation links in [NavigationServer2D] and [NavigationServer3D].

values.name: NAVIGATION_OBSTACLE_COUNT
values.summary: Number of active navigation obstacles in the [NavigationServer2D] and [NavigationServer3D].

values.name: NAVIGATION_POLYGON_COUNT
values.summary: Number of navigation mesh polygons in [NavigationServer2D] and [NavigationServer3D].

values.name: NAVIGATION_REGION_COUNT
values.summary: Number of active navigation regions in [NavigationServer2D] and [NavigationServer3D].

values.name: OBJECT_COUNT
values.summary: Number of objects currently instantiated (including nodes). [i]Lower is better.[/i]

values.name: OBJECT_NODE_COUNT
values.summary: Number of nodes currently instantiated in the scene tree. This also includes the root node. [i]Lower is better.[/i]

values.name: OBJECT_ORPHAN_NODE_COUNT
values.summary: Number of orphan nodes, i.e. nodes which are not parented to a node of the scene tree. [i]Lower is better.[/i] [b]Note:[/b] This is only available in debug mode and will always return [code]0[/code] when used in a project exported in release mode.

values.name: OBJECT_RESOURCE_COUNT
values.summary: Number of resources currently used. [i]Lower is better.[/i]

values.name: PHYSICS_2D_ACTIVE_OBJECTS
values.summary: Number of active [RigidBody2D] nodes in the game. [i]Lower is better.[/i]

values.name: PHYSICS_2D_COLLISION_PAIRS
values.summary: Number of collision pairs in the 2D physics engine. [i]Lower is better.[/i]

values.name: PHYSICS_2D_ISLAND_COUNT
values.summary: Number of islands in the 2D physics engine. [i]Lower is better.[/i]

values.name: PHYSICS_3D_ACTIVE_OBJECTS
values.summary: Number of active [RigidBody3D] and [VehicleBody3D] nodes in the game. [i]Lower is better.[/i]

values.name: PHYSICS_3D_COLLISION_PAIRS
values.summary: Number of collision pairs in the 3D physics engine. [i]Lower is better.[/i]

values.name: PHYSICS_3D_ISLAND_COUNT
values.summary: Number of islands in the 3D physics engine. [i]Lower is better.[/i]

values.name: PIPELINE_COMPILATIONS_CANVAS
values.summary: Number of pipeline compilations that were triggered by the 2D canvas renderer.

values.name: PIPELINE_COMPILATIONS_DRAW
values.summary: Number of pipeline compilations that were triggered while drawing the scene. These compilations will show up as stutters during gameplay the first time a user runs the game and the pipeline is required.

values.name: PIPELINE_COMPILATIONS_MESH
values.summary: Number of pipeline compilations that were triggered by loading meshes. These compilations will show up as longer loading times the first time a user runs the game and the pipeline is required.

values.name: PIPELINE_COMPILATIONS_SPECIALIZATION
values.summary: Number of pipeline compilations that were triggered to optimize the current scene. These compilations are done in the background and should not cause any stutters whatsoever.

values.name: PIPELINE_COMPILATIONS_SURFACE
values.summary: Number of pipeline compilations that were triggered by building the surface cache before rendering the scene. These compilations will show up as a stutter when loading a scene the first time a user runs the game and the pipeline is required.

values.name: RENDER_BUFFER_MEM_USED
values.summary: The amount of render buffer memory used (in bytes). [i]Lower is better.[/i]

values.name: RENDER_TEXTURE_MEM_USED
values.summary: The amount of texture memory used (in bytes). [i]Lower is better.[/i]

values.name: RENDER_TOTAL_DRAW_CALLS_IN_FRAME
values.summary: The total number of draw calls performed in the last rendered frame. This metric doesn't include culled objects (either via hiding nodes, frustum culling or occlusion culling), since they do not result in draw calls. [i]Lower is better.[/i]

values.name: RENDER_TOTAL_OBJECTS_IN_FRAME
values.summary: The total number of objects in the last rendered frame. This metric doesn't include culled objects (either via hiding nodes, frustum culling or occlusion culling). [i]Lower is better.[/i]

values.name: RENDER_TOTAL_PRIMITIVES_IN_FRAME
values.summary: The total number of vertices or indices rendered in the last rendered frame. This metric doesn't include primitives from culled objects (either via hiding nodes, frustum culling or occlusion culling). Due to the depth prepass and shadow passes, the number of primitives is always higher than the actual number of vertices in the scene (typically double or triple the original vertex count). [i]Lower is better.[/i]

values.name: RENDER_VIDEO_MEM_USED
values.summary: The amount of video memory used (texture and vertex memory combined, in bytes). Since this metric also includes miscellaneous allocations, this value is always greater than the sum of [constant RENDER_TEXTURE_MEM_USED] and [constant RENDER_BUFFER_MEM_USED]. [i]Lower is better.[/i]

values.name: TIME_FPS
values.summary: The number of frames rendered in the last second. This metric is only updated once per second, even if queried more often. [i]Higher is better.[/i]

values.name: TIME_NAVIGATION_PROCESS
values.summary: Time it took to complete one navigation step, in seconds. This includes navigation map updates as well as agent avoidance calculations. [i]Lower is better.[/i]

values.name: TIME_PHYSICS_PROCESS
values.summary: Time it took to complete one physics frame, in seconds. [i]Lower is better.[/i]

values.name: TIME_PROCESS
values.summary: Time it took to complete one frame, in seconds. [i]Lower is better.[/i]

values.name: MONITOR_TYPE_MEMORY
values.summary: Monitor output is formatted as computer memory. Submitted values should represent a number of bytes.

values.name: MONITOR_TYPE_PERCENTAGE
values.summary: Monitor output is formatted as a percentage. Submitted values should represent a fractional value rather than the percentage directly, e.g. [code]0.5[/code] for [code]50.00%[/code].

values.name: MONITOR_TYPE_QUANTITY
values.summary: Monitor output is formatted as an integer value.

values.name: MONITOR_TYPE_TIME
values.summary: Monitor output is formatted as time in milliseconds. Submitted values should represent a time in seconds (not milliseconds).

constants.name: TIME_FPS
constants.summary: The number of frames rendered in the last second. This metric is only updated once per second, even if queried more often. [i]Higher is better.[/i]

constants.name: TIME_PROCESS
constants.summary: Time it took to complete one frame, in seconds. [i]Lower is better.[/i]

constants.name: TIME_PHYSICS_PROCESS
constants.summary: Time it took to complete one physics frame, in seconds. [i]Lower is better.[/i]

constants.name: TIME_NAVIGATION_PROCESS
constants.summary: Time it took to complete one navigation step, in seconds. This includes navigation map updates as well as agent avoidance calculations. [i]Lower is better.[/i]

constants.name: MEMORY_STATIC
constants.summary: Static memory currently used, in bytes. Not available in release builds. [i]Lower is better.[/i]

constants.name: MEMORY_STATIC_MAX
constants.summary: Available static memory. Not available in release builds. [i]Lower is better.[/i]

constants.name: MEMORY_MESSAGE_BUFFER_MAX
constants.summary: Largest amount of memory the message queue buffer has used, in bytes. The message queue is used for deferred functions calls and notifications. [i]Lower is better.[/i]

constants.name: OBJECT_COUNT
constants.summary: Number of objects currently instantiated (including nodes). [i]Lower is better.[/i]

constants.name: OBJECT_RESOURCE_COUNT
constants.summary: Number of resources currently used. [i]Lower is better.[/i]

constants.name: OBJECT_NODE_COUNT
constants.summary: Number of nodes currently instantiated in the scene tree. This also includes the root node. [i]Lower is better.[/i]

constants.name: OBJECT_ORPHAN_NODE_COUNT
constants.summary: Number of orphan nodes, i.e. nodes which are not parented to a node of the scene tree. [i]Lower is better.[/i] [b]Note:[/b] This is only available in debug mode and will always return [code]0[/code] when used in a project exported in release mode.

constants.name: RENDER_TOTAL_OBJECTS_IN_FRAME
constants.summary: The total number of objects in the last rendered frame. This metric doesn't include culled objects (either via hiding nodes, frustum culling or occlusion culling). [i]Lower is better.[/i]

constants.name: RENDER_TOTAL_PRIMITIVES_IN_FRAME
constants.summary: The total number of vertices or indices rendered in the last rendered frame. This metric doesn't include primitives from culled objects (either via hiding nodes, frustum culling or occlusion culling). Due to the depth prepass and shadow passes, the number of primitives is always higher than the actual number of vertices in the scene (typically double or triple the original vertex count). [i]Lower is better.[/i]

constants.name: RENDER_TOTAL_DRAW_CALLS_IN_FRAME
constants.summary: The total number of draw calls performed in the last rendered frame. This metric doesn't include culled objects (either via hiding nodes, frustum culling or occlusion culling), since they do not result in draw calls. [i]Lower is better.[/i]

constants.name: RENDER_VIDEO_MEM_USED
constants.summary: The amount of video memory used (texture and vertex memory combined, in bytes). Since this metric also includes miscellaneous allocations, this value is always greater than the sum of [constant RENDER_TEXTURE_MEM_USED] and [constant RENDER_BUFFER_MEM_USED]. [i]Lower is better.[/i]

constants.name: RENDER_TEXTURE_MEM_USED
constants.summary: The amount of texture memory used (in bytes). [i]Lower is better.[/i]

constants.name: RENDER_BUFFER_MEM_USED
constants.summary: The amount of render buffer memory used (in bytes). [i]Lower is better.[/i]

constants.name: PHYSICS_2D_ACTIVE_OBJECTS
constants.summary: Number of active [RigidBody2D] nodes in the game. [i]Lower is better.[/i]

constants.name: PHYSICS_2D_COLLISION_PAIRS
constants.summary: Number of collision pairs in the 2D physics engine. [i]Lower is better.[/i]

constants.name: PHYSICS_2D_ISLAND_COUNT
constants.summary: Number of islands in the 2D physics engine. [i]Lower is better.[/i]

constants.name: PHYSICS_3D_ACTIVE_OBJECTS
constants.summary: Number of active [RigidBody3D] and [VehicleBody3D] nodes in the game. [i]Lower is better.[/i]

constants.name: PHYSICS_3D_COLLISION_PAIRS
constants.summary: Number of collision pairs in the 3D physics engine. [i]Lower is better.[/i]

constants.name: PHYSICS_3D_ISLAND_COUNT
constants.summary: Number of islands in the 3D physics engine. [i]Lower is better.[/i]

constants.name: AUDIO_OUTPUT_LATENCY
constants.summary: Output latency of the [AudioServer]. Equivalent to calling [method AudioServer.get_output_latency], it is not recommended to call this every frame.

constants.name: MONITOR_TYPE_QUANTITY
